target.name: NVision Imaging Technologies
target.founder: Martin Plenio, Ilai Schwartz, Fedor Jelezko, Alex Retzker

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
NVision Imaging Technologies is a Series A biotech company in the Biotech & Health sector that has raised $20M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 8 private peers and 3 public companies (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) at same or adjacent stages, including Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B Series B), Xaira Therapeutics ($2.7B Seed), and Neko Health ($1.8B Series B).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $40M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables produces a $1.3B implied valuation, with a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because NVision's revenue is not on file; the sector P/S median of 6.1x across three public tickers cannot be deployed without target-level sales data.

The triangulated range is $34M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median of $670M. This wide spread reflects meaningful stage and maturity dispersion within the peer set. The stage-progression floor suggests downside protection anchored to capital deployed; the peer median signals material upside if NVision's technology translates to clinical or commercial traction comparable to later-stage cohort peers. Key uncertainty is revenue trajectory and clinical validation progress relative to the reference set.
